signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD/CAD — NZD 1W avg: +0.629% (n=4) outperforming CAD -0.591% (n=3) with a G8 spread of 1.219%. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% — +150bp carry favors USD, but NZD carry advantage over CAD is driven by RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% — 0bp carry neutral. NZD long% at 23.0% (COT) and 25% long (retail) indicates room for further NZD appreciation. Watch for NZD/CAD to extend gains as NZD strength is supported by both G8 outperformance and potential RBNZ hike on 27 May.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.61 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains bid; Middle East tensions introduce intervention risk on a break above 160.00. JPY COT LF short −88,543 (crowded carry). Carry dominates until VIX > 25. Watch for USD/JPY to test 160.00 as Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — +300bp carry favors USD.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +48,299 (specs long) vs retail 10% long (extreme short) — institutional vs retail divergence. AUD/USD at 0.7238. Watch for AUD/USD to extend gains as RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% — -60bp carry disadvantage is offset by speculative long positioning and contrarian retail sentiment.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD COT LF net short -18,293 (specs short) — neutral positioning. EUR/USD at 1.1762. Watch for EUR/USD to test 1.18 as Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% — +175bp carry favors USD.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Election Risks
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.36 — election risks weigh on Pound. Watch for GBP/USD to test 1.35 as BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp carry neutral.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.596, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short −16.8k, setting up a divergence with the RBNZ's forward bias, which has a 40% hike priced in. This creates a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signaling. The unwind of these positions is likely to be catalyzed by the RBNZ meeting on 27 May.
